title: Orana Wildlife Park Review
meta_desc: Discover NZ's only open-range zoo, perfect for families with kids of all ages. Get insider tips, prices, and honest reviews to plan your visit to Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch.
intro: Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch is a must-visit for families, offering an unforgettable experience with its open-range zoo and unique animal encounters. With a price tag of $32 for adults and $14 for kids, it's an affordable day out for the whole family. But what's it really like with kids, and is it worth the cost?

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Orana Wildlife Park is home to a wide range of animals, including lions, giraffes, and rhinos, which roam freely in the African Savannah enclosure. A guided bus tour takes you through the enclosure, giving you a thrilling up-close experience. The park also features an excellent native wildlife section, where you can learn about and see NZ's unique animals.

The highlight for many kids (and adults!) is the daily giraffe feeding at 2pm. Be sure to arrive early to secure a spot, as it can get busy. The park is suitable for children of all ages, from 0+, making it an ideal destination for families with kids of different ages.
list: African Savannah enclosure with lions, giraffes, and rhinos
Native wildlife section with NZ animals

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
With prices starting at $14 for kids and $32 for adults, Orana Wildlife Park offers great value for money, considering the unique experience and animal encounters. To make the most of your visit, plan to spend around 3-4 hours at the park, and consider packing a picnic lunch to enjoy in the designated areas.

To save money, look out for special deals and discounts on the park's website, especially during off-peak seasons. You can also book your tickets online in advance to avoid waiting in line at the gate.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body:
The best time to visit Orana Wildlife Park is during the warmer months (October to April), when the weather is pleasant and the animals are more active. However, if you prefer smaller crowds, consider visiting during the week or during the off-peak season.

To book your tickets, visit the Orana Wildlife Park website and follow the online booking process. You can also purchase tickets at the gate, but booking in advance is recommended to avoid waiting in line.
list: Book tickets online in advance to avoid waiting in line
Visit during the warmer months for better weather and animal activity
Consider visiting during the week or off-peak season for smaller crowds

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While Orana Wildlife Park is an excellent destination for families, there are a few downsides to consider. The park can get busy, especially during peak season, so be prepared for crowds and potential waiting times. Additionally, some areas of the park may be closed due to weather or animal welfare reasons, so it's a good idea to check the park's website before your visit.

Despite these minor drawbacks, Orana Wildlife Park remains a top choice for families in Christchurch, offering an unforgettable and educational experience that's sure to delight kids of all ages.
